refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Optimize processing latency within teleoperation control systems.
FR3: Ensure timely delivery of telemetry and tasking data to support control decisions.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.
DP2: Implement edge computing on vehicle to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Design efficient telemetry and tasking data encoding and compression.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use low-latency network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation data.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network protocol and teleoperation control logic
coupling_check.notes: DP1 focuses on network protocol and QoS, which can be modularized separately from vehicle control logic, minimizing coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network conditions meet agreed parameters for latency measurement.
Risk that network QoS may be degraded by external factors beyond system control.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating edge computing (DP2) if network improvements alone do not meet latency requirements.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for teleoperation control logic, network communication management, and latency optimization.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Handles bidirectional communication between operator and vehicle for teleoperation data.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Sends vehicle state and sensor data to operator.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives operator commands for vehicle control.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope; contains teleoperation but also other functions, leading to higher diffusion.
Vehicle module contains physical components; teleoperation logic only partially contained.
Highest containment score for teleoperation DP; minimal diffusion risk.
Safety services focus on alerts and policies, not teleoperation latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning teleoperation latency DP to the Teleoperation module centralizes responsibility and minimizes diffusion, enabling focused latency optimization.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on network infrastructure outside module control.
Interface coordination with Vehicle and Safety Services modules must be well-defined.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Define clear interface contracts with Vehicle and Safety Services to mitigate cross-module dependencies.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (network protocol and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR latency requirement with measurable parameters.

diffusion_risks: Minimal diffusion risk as DP is contained within Teleoperation module.
Cross-module interface dependencies identified and manageable.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module has highest containment score and clear interface boundaries.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency verification can be performed via telemetry and network monitoring within Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency responsibility to Teleoperation module.

recommended_revisions: Clarify interface specifications between Teleoperation and Vehicle modules.
Establish monitoring mechanisms for network link performance.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition sufficiently refines the FR, selects a viable DP, and assigns it to an appropriate IM with manageable risks and clear verification paths.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Optimize processing latency within teleoperation control systems.
FR3: Ensure timely delivery of telemetry and tasking data to support control decisions.

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; decomposition and assignments are consistent and feasible.

assumptions: Network conditions remain within agreed parameters.
Teleoperation module can implement required network protocols and QoS.
open_risks: Interface coordination complexity with Vehicle and Safety Services.
evidence_needed: Latency measurements under operational network conditions.
